content:
6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Let’s talk about the thing nobody wants to say out loud when you’re on application number 180 and still haven’t heard back.
You’re not just competing with the other people in your city. You’re not even just competing with AI, though that’s a whole other article. You’re competing with a genuinely global talent pool where a solid mid-level developer in another country bills somewhere around $30 an hour, and a company that would’ve paid you $120k a couple of years ago is now doing some very cold arithmetic.
I want to be careful here, because this topic attracts two kinds of nonsense. One says offshore developers are cheap garbage that always needs rewriting. The other says they’re flawless drop-in replacements and you’re doomed. Both are wrong, and the truth in between is actually more useful to you than either.
The rate is real. The math is not as simple as the rate.
First, the number is legitimate. Offshore developer rates in 2026 genuinely range from roughly $18 to $75 an hour depending on the country and seniority. India and Vietnam sit at the low end. Eastern Europe runs higher, often $60 to $80 for senior talent. Latin America lands in the middle and gets a premium for time-zone overlap with…
